full_prompt: Design a single-part intake restrictor plate for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2025 car running a 710 cc four-stroke petrol engine on RON 100 fuel. Per the collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark rules inherited by collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark Local Rule 2nd Edition, all intake air must pass through a single circular orifice of exactly 20.0 mm diameter (for gasoline); the restrictor must be installed between the throttle and the engine, and must be a single rigid part with no moving pieces or bypass path. External envelope of the plate is 80 mm (outer diameter) x 20 mm thickness maximum. The central orifice is 20.0 mm diameter with a converging nozzle on the upstream face (included angle 21 deg) and a diverging nozzle on the downstream face (included angle 7 deg); the throat itself must be a parallel section of length 5 mm to ensure the 20.0 mm diameter is maintained for the minimum 5 mm per tech inspection. The plate is flanged to the throttle body with six M6 bolts on a 70 mm bolt circle diameter. Material is 2024-T3 aluminium with Young's modulus 73.1 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2780 kg/m^3, yield strength 345 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 483 MPa. Load cases: LC1 (Backfire overpressure) — internal transient gauge pressure of 0.35 MPa on the downstream face (representative of intake backfire peak for a 710 cc port-injected engine at 12,500 rpm redline) with the bolt flange fully fixed; LC2 (Steady vacuum) — internal gauge pressure of -0.08 MPa on the upstream face (WOT manifold vacuum) with bolt flange fully fixed; LC3 (Thermal) — uniform temperature rise of 80 K across the part (from 25 C ambient to 105 C heat-soaked bay) while bolt flange is fixed, simulating hot-bay soak; LC4 (Bolt preload) — each M6 bolt preloaded to 7 kN with ambient pressure. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, max von Mises must stay below 230 MPa (yield/1.5); (R2) under LC2, max von Mises must stay below 230 MPa; (R3) under LC3 + LC4 combined, max von Mises must stay below 230 MPa; (R4) throat diameter must not deform under LC1 by more than 0.05 mm (so effective throat diameter stays within 20.00 -0.05 / +0.05 mm tolerance band required by tech inspection); (R5) total restrictor mass must not exceed 90 g. Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for LC1, LC2, LC4 and a thermal-structural coupled linear analysis for LC3. Vibration analysis, fluid (CFD) analysis, and radiation analysis are explicitly not required even though real restrictor design typically uses CFD; this eval focuses on structural integrity of the fixed-geometry orifice only. Output one STEP AP242 solid body plus a report listing restrictor mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, throat diameter change under LC1 in mm, and combined stress under LC3 + LC4.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part intake restrictor plate for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2025 car running a 710 cc four-stroke RON 100 engine. Per FSJ 2025 Local Rule 2nd Ed, all intake air must pass through a single 20.0 mm gasoline orifice with a 5 mm parallel throat.

Envelope: 80 mm OD x <= 20 mm thickness; 21 deg upstream converging nozzle + 7 deg downstream diverging nozzle; six M6 bolts on a 70 mm bolt circle; 2024-T3 aluminum.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 on all static and thermal cases; throat dimensional tolerance 20.00 mm +/- 0.05 mm for tech inspection.

Load cases.
- Backfire overpressure: 0.35 MPa transient on the downstream face (bolt flange fixed).
- Steady vacuum: -0.08 MPa on the upstream face (WOT).
- Thermal: uniform 80 K rise (25 C ambient to 105 C heat-soaked bay) with flange fixed.
- Bolt preload: 7 kN per M6 combined with ambient pressure.

prompt.title: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2025 20 mm intake restrictor plate
prompt.brief: Single-part aluminium restrictor with a 20.0 mm throat, 70 mm bolt circle, rated for 0.35 MPa backfire, 80 K bay soak, and the tech-inspection throat tolerance of +/-0.05 mm.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Restrictor is not a rotating/vibratory part; collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark rule specifies static geometry only.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: CFD is common in practice but this eval isolates structural pass/fail of the fixed geometry.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Thin-shell buckling negligible for a 20 mm-thick disc of this size.

eval_coverage: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark-mandated fixed orifice geometry
strict geometric tolerance limit under load
